title: microchilus lamprophyllus
intro: Microchilus lamprophyllus, also known as the "Golden Chain Vine," is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to tropical regions of Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and stems have been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and providing antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be used to make ointments and salves to aid in wound healing.
